title: B.Sc. Medical Biotechnology (Specialization in Bioinformatics)
content: The B.Sc. Medical Biotechnology with Bioinformatics specialization is an interdisciplinary undergraduate program that integrates core biomedical sciences with computational and data-driven approaches. The curriculum provides strong foundations in molecular biology, genetics, biochemistry, microbiology, and human health, along with training in bioinformatics tools and biological data analysis. The program prepares students to understand disease mechanisms at the molecular level and apply computational methods in diagnostics, genomics, and biomedical research. Graduates are equipped for entry-level roles in biotechnology and healthcare industries, research laboratories, diagnostic centres, and are well-prepared for higher studies in medical biotechnology, bioinformatics, and related disciplines. XII with Science with minimum 50% marks. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list Graduates of the program are prepared for the following roles: Key recruiting sectors include: Graduates are also well-positioned for doctoral studies and advanced research careers in biomedical and computational sciences. 01 The B.Sc. Medical Biotechnology program (specialization in Bioinformatics) aims to develop skilled professionals with interdisciplinary expertise in molecular biology, genomics, computational biology, and biomedical data analysis. 02 The program focuses on integrating biotechnology with bioinformatics tools to understand biological systems, analyze omics data, support drug discovery, disease diagnostics, and personalized medicine approaches, while preparing students for careers in biomedical research, healthcare industries, and computational life science sectors. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 Communication: Communicate effectively with the Medical & Healthcare community. 09 The Medical Biotechnology and society: Apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety and legal issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the Medical Biotechnology practices. 10 Environment and sustainability: Understand the impact of the Medical Biotechnology solution in societal and environmental contexts, demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for Bio Waste Management. 11 01 02 03 01 Regulatory and Global Healthcare Perspectives: Evaluate critical domestic and global regulatory frameworks and healthcare challenges that influence biomedical and biotechnology product development. 02 Biotechnology Research and Data Analysis Skills: Demonstrate competency in conducting biotechnology research, including experimental design, data evaluation, analysis, interpretation, and scientific presentation of research outcomes. 03 Professional Communication and Regulatory Collaboration: Effectively communicate and collaborate with biotechnology service providers, healthcare professionals, and regulatory agencies to develop culturally competent domestic and global strategies in medical biotechnology. 04 Ethical, Social, and Professional Responsibility in Biotechnology: Demonstrate awareness of regulatory compliance, ethical principles, and social responsibilities essential for professional practice in global biotechnology research environments.
